title: Shaders 101
description: Write your first Metal shaders using the Metal Shading Language.

sections.title: Shader Setup
sections.expectedOutput:
Metal Shader Pipeline Structure:

[Shader code listing]

--- Swift Setup ---

[Swift setup code listing]
sections.solution:
import Foundation
import Metal

let device = MTLCreateSystemDefaultDevice()!
print("Using Metal device: \(device.name)")

sections.question: What does the '[[position]]' attribute indicate in Metal?
options: That the output is a fragment shader input
sections.explanation: '[[position]]' marks a vertex output as input to the fragment shader, defining the pixel's position.
